abl_targeted_boundary.decision: do not widen ABL reverse engineering until a header/handoff contradiction appears
unverified: final branch into the candidate kernel entry point
verified: boot and vendor_boot header v4 are parsed from pinned stock images
vendor_boot carries load addresses, DTB, vendor ramdisk, cmdline, and bootconfig
retained ABL log says authentication failed but custom kernel binary is allowed
retained ABL log says unlocked device skips boot verification
ABL proceeds to hypervisor and DT selection messages after the warning
the same retained ABL boot reaches Shutting Down UEFI Boot Services after the warning
ABL records reboot_reason values consumed on the next boot

markers: no bootloop during the full 120 second park window
Device is unlocked, Skipping boot verification

init_selection.requested_init_override: init= is attempted after ramdisk init failure

ordered_flow: ABL selects boot/vendor_boot/dtb and prepares handoff
kernel_init_freeable waits for kthreadd and runs initcalls
rootfs_initcall populate_rootfs unpacks built-in and external initramfs
init_eaccess(/init) selects initramfs or prepare_namespace fallback
kernel_init finalizes init memory and calls run_init_process

pid1_failure.ramdisk_exec_failure: log error, then continue to requested/default fallback
pid1_failure.v3432_gap: first retained marker was gated behind module insert and platform probe

pre_exec_security_steps: free init memory and mark read-only

next_host_units: design the stock-init child service supervisor protocol and prerequisite bundle
design mount-namespace plus pivot_root handoff without claiming Debian PID 1
host-test child subreaping, mount isolation, and reversible device ownership
separately evaluate a kernel-entry witness without reusing direct-PID1 retained-marker live gates

observation_channels.activation: built-in support plus bootargs earlycon/console and routed UART
observation_channels.claim_limit: absence of UART output proves nothing while disabled
observation_channels.earliest_stage: kernel entry before normal console init
observation_channels.evidence: CONFIG_SERIAL_EARLYCON=y but live cmdline has console=null and nohyp_uart
observation_channels.name: earlycon_uart

observation_channels.activation: CONFIG_PSTORE_RAM plus enabled ramoops reserved-memory DT node
observation_channels.claim_limit: empty pstore is not kernel/PID1 non-entry proof
observation_channels.evidence: pstore is built-in but live ramoops_region status is disabled
observation_channels.name: ramoops_pstore

observation_channels.activation: exact module ABI, samsung,kernel_log_buf DT match, reserved memory, procfs
observation_channels.claim_limit: cannot witness kernel entry or PID1 code before module load/probe
observation_channels.earliest_stage: stock first-stage PID1 module load position 2 and successful platform probe
observation_channels.evidence: source verified, live bound, creates /proc/last_kmsg and /proc/ap_klog
observation_channels.name: sec_log_buf

observation_channels.activation: samsung,sec_debug DT match, MID/debug parameters, panic notifier registration
observation_channels.claim_limit: not the retained printk-ring owner and unavailable before its module load
observation_channels.earliest_stage: stock first-stage PID1 module load position 105 and successful platform probe
observation_channels.evidence: source verified, live bound, sysrq panic positive control
observation_channels.name: sec_debug

observation_channels.activation: PMIC/PON hardware reset state and bootloader RDX classifier
observation_channels.claim_limit: classifies reset cause; does not identify the last Linux instruction or prove PID1
observation_channels.earliest_stage: reset latch consumed by XBL/ABL before Linux
observation_channels.evidence: operator RDX PMIC abnormal reset plus reset_reason=MPON/reboot_reason records
observation_channels.name: pmic_pon_reset_reason

observation_channels.activation: O1.1 Magisk overlay service with bounded DR-daemon handoff
observation_channels.claim_limit: proves stock-first-stage control, not direct-PID1 USB
observation_channels.earliest_stage: after stock first-stage modules, gadget, and sys.usb.configured
observation_channels.name: stock_usb_tty_control

selected_architecture.control_plane: retain O1.1-proven ttyGS0 framed channel outside the Debian mount namespace
selected_architecture.debian_root: new private mount namespace plus pivot_root; not chroot
selected_architecture.direct_pid1_track: only path to true Debian PID 1 on this kernel; research-only until a pre-userspace witness exists
selected_architecture.failure_behavior: return control to stock init and keep USB recovery channel
selected_architecture.global_pid1: retain stock Android init as hardware/watchdog/recovery owner
handoff_gates: sec_log_buf driver bound and both proc nodes present
watchdog modules registered and kernel pet path alive
stock UDC/gadget configured and framed USB roundtrip passes
Debian rootfs identity and read-only preflight pass
DRM/input/audio ownership transition plan is explicit and reversible
selected_architecture.kernel_constraint: CONFIG_PID_NS=n and CONFIG_USER_NS=n prohibit namespace PID 1
selected_architecture.kernel_rebuild_track: CONFIG_PID_NS enablement is a separate research track, not a deployment prerequisite
selected_architecture.name: stock_global_pid1_with_mount_namespace_service_supervisor
selected_architecture.native_runtime: launch a native supervisor after a device-reported prerequisite bundle
selected_architecture.process_model: custom child service supervisor; Debian init is not PID 1
selected_architecture.reaping_model: supervisor becomes a child subreaper and directly owns approved services

watchdog.direct_pid1_requirement: load proven watchdog closure before any unbounded park, or remain under stock init ownership
watchdog.hardware_state: pre-armed before stock userspace; PMIC/PON abnormal reset near 30 s when unmanaged
watchdog.kernel_core: built-in watchdog core auto-pings a registered running watchdog
watchdog.live_discriminator: M31B loaded stock watchdog closure and survived 120 seconds

stock_first_stage.open_timeout_meaning: infinite kernel care until userspace takeover
